rootCause.summary: Hero Accept used shared media upload + sync pipeline, causing Hero assets to flow through shared vault/feed systems.
evidence: Hero accept previously called uploadMedia(formData) and syncFromVault(true).
syncFromVault ingests thumbnails/videos into shared keys and invokes AI_CLEANUP_AGENT sync/distribution.

rootCause.summary: Hero vault inventory was sourced from shared media keys rather than Hero-only keys.
evidence: Hero Manager asset registry consumed loadHeroVaultItems(), so inventory was merged by design.

rootCause.summary: Hero persistence used multiple authorities (manager config + legacy hero keys) with non-deterministic precedence.
evidence: hydrateHeroBackgroundStores evaluated persisted hero keys and manager config in competing paths.
Could restore stale values after refresh/restart.

before: Accept -> uploadMedia(/api/reels) -> websocket CREATED -> syncFromVault -> shared vault writes (personal_video_vault/personal_thumbnails) -> AI_CLEANUP_AGENT feed/placeholder sync.
Hero inventory -> loadHeroVaultItems() merged shared vault keys.
Persistence load -> mixed manager config and legacy keys with conflicting precedence.
after: Accept -> local hero store update (HERO_POSTER_IMAGE/HERO_BACKGROUND_VIDEO) -> saveHeroManagerConfig -> hero-manager-updated event only.
Hero inventory -> loadHeroVaultItems() reads Hero-only keys (reelforge_hero_image/reelforge_hero_video) and maps to heroAssetId.
Hydrate load -> manager-config custom source applied first, then legacy/default fallback.

assertions: Hero vault item appears immediately after Hero save path (vaultItemsCount=1).
After reload, manager config and hero vault item remain present.
Shared vault/feed counts remain unchanged in validation run.
